name: City of London (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the cross of St George and the sword of St Paul

description: A white field bearing the red St George's Cross, with the sword of St Paul (a red sword pointing upward) in the upper-hoist canton.
history: The City of London's banner of arms — a defaced English cross of St George — has been used since at least the early 17th century; the sword recalls St Paul, patron saint of the City. Used solely by the Square Mile (the historic City of London), distinct from any flag of Greater London.

features.symbolism: the St George's Cross of England
St Paul, patron saint of London (whose cathedral stands in the City); legend holds that the sword represents the weapon used to behead him
